channel.title: Finance Pulse
channel.description: A daily banking finance-function intelligence briefing: macro, banking, regulatory, and AI in finance, built for client conversations.
channel.itunes:author: Shawn Mascia
channel.itunes:summary: A daily banking finance-function intelligence briefing: macro, banking, regulatory, and AI in finance, built for client conversations.

item.title: Finance Pulse - Jun 3, 2026
item.description:
Now I have sufficient material to produce both parts. Let me compile the briefing.
---
# Finance Pulse | Wednesday, June 3, 2026
**Bottom line: Super-regional banks enter Q2 earnings season in a NIM tightening vise, with rates frozen, energy-driven inflation above three percent, a re-proposed Basel III Endgame comment clock ticking to June 18, and a genuine agentic AI deployment wave accelerating faster than governance frameworks can keep up.**
---
## Top Takeaways
1. The Fed kept the federal funds rate unchanged at the three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent target range for a third consecutive meeting in April 2026. The decision was not unanimous, with one member voting to lower rates by twenty-five basis points and three others objecting to language suggesting the central bank would eventually resume cutting, marking the first four-dissent FOMC vote since October 1992.
2. The Bureau of Economic Analysis showed March PCE accelerating, with headline PCE running at three-point-five percent year over year and core PCE at three-point-two percent, driven largely by energy costs tied to the ongoing Middle East conflict.
3. On March 19, 2026, the Fed, OCC, and FDIC issued three re-proposed rules comprehensively overhauling the existing US bank capital framework and implementing Basel III Endgame. Comments on all three proposals are due by June 18, 2026.
4. Q1 earnings from PNC, KeyCorp, U.S. Bancorp, Truist, Fifth Third, and Regions showed commercial lending gaining momentum, fee-based businesses carrying more weight, and technology investments beginning to reshape how banks compete for client relationships.
5. BlackLine CEO Owen Ryan reported Q1 2026 results and stated the firm is "defining the future of the financial close with Agentic Financial Operations," with growing adoption of Verity AI and Studio360, positioning BlackLine as "the essential governance layer for the AI era."
---
## Key Themes
### 1. Rates Frozen, Curve Steepening, NIM Vise Tightening *(Evolving)*
J.P. Morgan Global Research sees the Fed holding rates steady for the rest of 2026, with the next move likely being a hike of twenty-five basis points in the third quarter of 2027. The Middle East conflict has been a key driver of asset price movements, with two-year and ten-year Treasury yields rising further and near-term inflation compensation rising over the intermeeting period. For super-regionals, NIM fell sharply in April across both floating and fixed-rate structures, with SOFR NIM falling sixteen basis points to one-point-six-eight percent. The funding curve continued to steepen, pushing all-in funding costs higher across loan types, while deposit rates remained nearly flat, leaving bankers in a tightening vise between rising costs and stagnant revenue.
### 2. Basel III Endgame Re-Proposal: Comment Deadline This Month *(New)*
The agencies anticipate that overall capital in the banking system "would modestly decrease" if the proposals are implemented, and the FRB voted six-to-one to advance all three proposals, with Governor Michael Barr as the sole dissenting vote, signaling broad bipartisan support. Bank capital requirements are expected to decrease due to lower minimum risk weights, less conservative methodologies, and a narrower market risk framework, though the 2026 capital rules also add complexity and will require careful implementation. A final rule is highly probable, with implementation beginning as soon as 2027.
### 3. Agentic AI: From POC to Production, Governance Gap Widening *(Evolving)*
According to Wolters Kluwer, forty-four percent of finance teams will use agentic AI in 2026, representing an increase of over six hundred percent. Yet ninety-nine percent of companies plan to put agents into production but only eleven percent have done so, due to implementation challenges related to data, governance, and security. Accenture analysis shows roughly one-third of financial services firms have scaled AI for core processes, and those that have are already seeing outsized returns and accelerating investment. McKinsey notes that AI pioneers are set to gain a four percent return on tangible equity advantage, while slow movers risk being stuck with an uncompetitive cost base.
---
## Banking Finance-Function
**Super-Regional Snapshot (Q1 2026):**
U.S. Bancorp highlighted sustained fee-based growth tied to merchant services, small business cards, and embedded payment tools; KeyCorp reported a twelve percent increase in priority fee-based businesses, including commercial payments and investment banking. At Fifth Third, commercial payment fees reached two-hundred-and-eighteen million dollars, with management noting increased adoption of managed services solutions. Truist reported commercial-led loan growth, with average loans held for investment rising two-point-three billion dollars, driven by one-point-eight percent growth in commercial loans, even as consumer balances declined.
**Deposit and Funding Dynamics:**
With rising funding costs in the wholesale market, deposit rates showed little movement in April; interest-bearing non-time deposit rates were down two basis points in both the Regional+ segment at two-point-six-four percent and Community segment at one-point-nine-eight percent. Community deposit portfolio balances are up approximately eight percent year-to-date indexed to December 2025, while Regional+ balances are roughly flat, meaning institutions may need to rely more on wholesale funding to support loan activity, with NIM implications.
**NIM Outlook:**
The 2026 NIM outlook, given stable rates and continued deposit repricing, is generally for modest further expansion or flat margins, and 2026 may be the first year since the rate-hike cycle where year-over-year NIM comparisons are relatively flat. Industry NIM currently averages approximately three-point-three-nine percent per FDIC data.
---
## Regulatory Radar
**Basel III Endgame Re-Proposal:** The June 18 comment deadline is the single most urgent date on every super-regional treasurer's and controller's calendar. To capture the benefits of the updated Basel III operational risk framework, banks will need to reassess internal models, governance frameworks, and capital planning processes, especially where operational risk capital remains a meaningful driver of strategy.
**DFAST 2026:** The Federal Reserve finalized hypothetical scenarios for its 2026 annual stress test and voted to maintain current stress test-related capital requirements until public feedback can be considered. The Fed is also pursuing enhanced scenario transparency with a new publicly disclosed macro model for stress testing. OCC requires covered institutions to publish company-run DFAST summaries between June 15 and July 15.
**Next FOMC:** The next FOMC meeting is scheduled for Tuesday and Wednesday, June 16 and 17, 2026. BofA Global Research now expects the Fed to remain on hold for the rest of this year, with two quarter-point cuts in July and September 2027.
**New Fed Chair:** The Justice Department said it would halt its criminal investigation into Powell, removing a key obstacle to the Senate's confirmation of his nominated successor, Kevin Warsh, whose appointment is set for May 15. Warsh's actual tenure and policy posture are the dominant rate-path wildcard for bank ALM desks through year-end.
---
## AI in Finance
**Real Deployments Worth Watching:**
On May 4, 2026, FIS announced it is working with Anthropic to bring agentic AI to banking, beginning with a Financial Crimes AI Agent. BMO and Amalgamated Bank are in development with the agent, with broader availability planned for the second half of 2026. The agent will compress AML alert and case investigations from days to minutes, reduce false positives, and enhance SAR narrative quality.
Oracle Financial Services, at its February 2026 summit in New York, announced an enterprise-class suite of AI-infused applications and pre-built AI agents, transforming banking with intelligent, conversational interfaces and autonomous agents.
**Controllership and Close Platforms:**
BlackLine is framing its Verity AI and Studio360 as "Agentic Financial Operations," positioning itself as the essential governance layer for the AI era for CFOs. OneStream was named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Financial Close and Consolidation Solutions. Gartner projects that sixty-two percent of cloud ERP spending will go to AI-enabled solutions by 2027, up from just fourteen percent in 2024.
**Governance Reality Check:**
The win condition for agentic AI in finance is not autonomous close; it is finance that compounds the controls. A modest set of well-instrumented agents that strengthen the audit trail beats a beautiful demo of autonomous close that the auditor refuses to sign off on.
**FP&A Accuracy Benchmark:**
The AFP Treasury Benchmarking Survey found that organizations using AI-assisted treasury and cashflow tools achieve eighty-eight to ninety-two percent forecast accuracy at a thirteen-week horizon, compared to sixty percent for organizations using manual or semi-automated methods.
---
## CFO Agenda, FP&A, and Transformation Signals
- **Capital planning refresh:** The Basel III Endgame re-proposal, combined with the June 16-17 FOMC meeting and the DFAST results cycle running June through July, compresses a major regulatory decision window into a single four-week period. CFOs and treasurers need capital plan sensitivity tables ready now. - **Rate scenario planning:** Both upside risks to inflation and downside risks to employment remain, pulling the interest rate outlook in opposite directions, with J.P. Morgan seeing rates on hold through 2026 and a possible hike in Q3 2027. Finance transformation teams should be stress-testing NIM and NII models for a rate-hold-then-hike scenario, not just the prior rate-cut consensus. - **Finance function AI operating model:** A fifth role is emerging in mature agentic AI programs: the finance agent owner, often sitting under the controller or FP&A lead, responsible for prompt curation, model-update review, and the evidence pipeline. This role does not exist in traditional finance org charts, and teams that absorb it into existing roles often see it deprioritized under cycle pressure. - **Workforce signal:** Visionaries now anticipate the rise of the "ten-times bank," where a single individual leads a team of AI co-workers to deliver exponentially greater output, with growth no longer constrained by headcount but by an organization's ability to reinvent work.
---
## Contrarian Insight
The consensus view is that the Basel III Endgame re-proposal is categorically "capital friendly" for super-regionals. The 2026 capital rules also add complexity and will require careful implementation, and the operational risk model changes, internal model governance overhaul, and new output floor mechanics may consume more finance and risk capacity than the modest capital relief is worth. Super-regionals with lean capital planning teams may actually face higher total cost of compliance than their megabank peers despite technically facing lower capital requirements.
---
## Client Conversation Hooks
1. **"With SOFR NIM down sixteen basis points in April alone and the funding curve still steepening, what is your current NIM sensitivity to a further twenty-five basis points of wholesale funding cost increase, and does your FP&A model update that in real time or monthly?"**
2. **"The Basel III Endgame comment deadline is June 18 -- two weeks away. Have you modeled both the capital release scenario and the operational risk model complexity scenario, and are your regulatory capital planning systems ready for a 2027 implementation start?"**
3. **"BlackLine is calling its Q1 2026 strategy 'Agentic Financial Operations' and OneStream just took the Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader position for close and consolidation. When your bank's CFO asks why the finance close still takes eight days, what is your transformation roadmap answer?"**
---
item.itunes:summary:
Now I have sufficient material to produce both parts. Let me compile the briefing.
---
# Finance Pulse | Wednesday, June 3, 2026
**Bottom line: Super-regional banks enter Q2 earnings season in a NIM tightening vise, with rates frozen, energy-driven inflation above three percent, a re-proposed Basel III Endgame comment clock ticking to June 18, and a genuine agentic AI deployment wave accelerating faster than governance frameworks can keep up.**
---
## Top Takeaways
1. The Fed kept the federal funds rate unchanged at the three-and-a-half to three-and-three-quarters percent target range for a third consecutive meeting in April 2026. The decision was not unanimous, with one member voting to lower rates by twenty-five basis points and three others objecting to language suggesting the central bank would eventually resume cutting, marking the first four-dissent FOMC vote since October 1992.
2. The Bureau of Economic Analysis showed March PCE accelerating, with headline PCE running at three-point-five percent year over year and core PCE at three-point-two percent, driven largely by energy costs tied to the ongoing Middle East conflict.
3. On March 19, 2026, the Fed, OCC, and FDIC issued three re-proposed rules comprehensively overhauling the existing US bank capital framework and implementing Basel III Endgame. Comments on all three proposals are due by June 18, 2026.
4. Q1 earnings from PNC, KeyCorp, U.S. Bancorp, Truist, Fifth Third, and Regions showed commercial lending gaining momentum, fee-based businesses carrying more weight, and technology investments beginning to reshape how banks compete for client relationships.
5. BlackLine CEO Owen Ryan reported Q1 2026 results and stated the firm is "defining the future of the financial close with Agentic Financial Operations," with growing adoption of Verity AI and Studio360, positioning BlackLine as "the essential governance layer for the AI era."
---
## Key Themes
### 1. Rates Frozen, Curve Steepening, NIM Vise Tightening *(Evolving)*
J.P. Morgan Global Research sees the Fed holding rates steady for the rest of 2026, with the next move likely being a hike of twenty-five basis points in the third quarter of 2027. The Middle East conflict has been a key driver of asset price movements, with two-year and ten-year Treasury yields rising further and near-term inflation compensation rising over the intermeeting period. For super-regionals, NIM fell sharply in April across both floating and fixed-rate structures, with SOFR NIM falling sixteen basis points to one-point-six-eight percent. The funding curve continued to steepen, pushing all-in funding costs higher across loan types, while deposit rates remained nearly flat, leaving bankers in a tightening vise between rising costs and stagnant revenue.
### 2. Basel III Endgame Re-Proposal: Comment Deadline This Month *(New)*
The agencies anticipate that overall capital in the banking system "would modestly decrease" if the proposals are implemented, and the FRB voted six-to-one to advance all three proposals, with Governor Michael Barr as the sole dissenting vote, signaling broad bipartisan support. Bank capital requirements are expected to decrease due to lower minimum risk weights, less conservative methodologies, and a narrower market risk framework, though the 2026 capital rules also add complexity and will require careful implementation. A final rule is highly probable, with implementation beginning as soon as 2027.
### 3. Agentic AI: From POC to Production, Governance Gap Widening *(Evolving)*
According to Wolters Kluwer, forty-four percent of finance teams will use agentic AI in 2026, representing an increase of over six hundred percent. Yet ninety-nine percent of companies plan to put agents into production but only eleven percent have done so, due to implementation challenges related to data, governance, and security. Accenture analysis shows roughly one-third of financial services firms have scaled AI for core processes, and those that have are already seeing outsized returns and accelerating investment. McKinsey notes that AI pioneers are set to gain a four percent return on tangible equity advantage, while slow movers risk being stuck with an uncompetitive cost base.
---
## Banking Finance-Function
**Super-Regional Snapshot (Q1 2026):**
U.S. Bancorp highlighted sustained fee-based growth tied to merchant services, small business cards, and embedded payment tools; KeyCorp reported a twelve percent increase in priority fee-based businesses, including commercial payments and investment banking. At Fifth Third, commercial payment fees reached two-hundred-and-eighteen million dollars, with management noting increased adoption of managed services solutions. Truist reported commercial-led loan growth, with average loans held for investment rising two-point-three billion dollars, driven by one-point-eight percent growth in commercial loans, even as consumer balances declined.
**Deposit and Funding Dynamics:**
With rising funding costs in the wholesale market, deposit rates showed little movement in April; interest-bearing non-time deposit rates were down two basis points in both the Regional+ segment at two-point-six-four percent and Community segment at one-point-nine-eight percent. Community deposit portfolio balances are up approximately eight percent year-to-date indexed to December 2025, while Regional+ balances are roughly flat, meaning institutions may need to rely more on wholesale funding to support loan activity, with NIM implications.
**NIM Outlook:**
The 2026 NIM outlook, given stable rates and continued deposit repricing, is generally for modest further expansion or flat margins, and 2026 may be the first year since the rate-hike cycle where year-over-year NIM comparisons are relatively flat. Industry NIM currently averages approximately three-point-three-nine percent per FDIC data.
---
## Regulatory Radar
**Basel III Endgame Re-Proposal:** The June 18 comment deadline is the single most urgent date on every super-regional treasurer's and controller's calendar. To capture the benefits of the updated Basel III operational risk framework, banks will need to reassess internal models, governance frameworks, and capital planning processes, especially where operational risk capital remains a meaningful driver of strategy.
**DFAST 2026:** The Federal Reserve finalized hypothetical scenarios for its 2026 annual stress test and voted to maintain current stress test-related capital requirements until public feedback can be considered. The Fed is also pursuing enhanced scenario transparency with a new publicly disclosed macro model for stress testing. OCC requires covered institutions to publish company-run DFAST summaries between June 15 and July 15.
**Next FOMC:** The next FOMC meeting is scheduled for Tuesday and Wednesday, June 16 and 17, 2026. BofA Global Research now expects the Fed to remain on hold for the rest of this year, with two quarter-point cuts in July and September 2027.
**New Fed Chair:** The Justice Department said it would halt its criminal investigation into Powell, removing a key obstacle to the Senate's confirmation of his nominated successor, Kevin Warsh, whose appointment is set for May 15. Warsh's actual tenure and policy posture are the dominant rate-path wildcard for bank ALM desks through year-end.
---
## AI in Finance
**Real Deployments Worth Watching:**
On May 4, 2026, FIS announced it is working with Anthropic to bring agentic AI to banking, beginning with a Financial Crimes AI Agent. BMO and Amalgamated Bank are in development with the agent, with broader availability planned for the second half of 2026. The agent will compress AML alert and case investigations from days to minutes, reduce false positives, and enhance SAR narrative quality.
Oracle Financial Services, at its February 2026 summit in New York, announced an enterprise-class suite of AI-infused applications and pre-built AI agents, transforming banking with intelligent, conversational interfaces and autonomous agents.
**Controllership and Close Platforms:**
BlackLine is framing its Verity AI and Studio360 as "Agentic Financial Operations," positioning itself as the essential governance layer for the AI era for CFOs. OneStream was named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Financial Close and Consolidation Solutions. Gartner projects that sixty-two percent of cloud ERP spending will go to AI-enabled solutions by 2027, up from just fourteen percent in 2024.
**Governance Reality Check:**
The win condition for agentic AI in finance is not autonomous close; it is finance that compounds the controls. A modest set of well-instrumented agents that strengthen the audit trail beats a beautiful demo of autonomous close that the auditor refuses to sign off on.
**FP&A Accuracy Benchmark:**
The AFP Treasury Benchmarking Survey found that organizations using AI-assisted treasury and cashflow tools achieve eighty-eight to ninety-two percent forecast accuracy at a thirteen-week horizon, compared to sixty percent for organizations using manual or semi-automated methods.
---
## CFO Agenda, FP&A, and Transformation Signals
- **Capital planning refresh:** The Basel III Endgame re-proposal, combined with the June 16-17 FOMC meeting and the DFAST results cycle running June through July, compresses a major regulatory decision window into a single four-week period. CFOs and treasurers need capital plan sensitivity tables ready now. - **Rate scenario planning:** Both upside risks to inflation and downside risks to employment remain, pulling the interest rate outlook in opposite directions, with J.P. Morgan seeing rates on hold through 2026 and a possible hike in Q3 2027. Finance transformation teams should be stress-testing NIM and NII models for a rate-hold-then-hike scenario, not just the prior rate-cut consensus. - **Finance function AI operating model:** A fifth role is emerging in mature agentic AI programs: the finance agent owner, often sitting under the controller or FP&A lead, responsible for prompt curation, model-update review, and the evidence pipeline. This role does not exist in traditional finance org charts, and teams that absorb it into existing roles often see it deprioritized under cycle pressure. - **Workforce signal:** Visionaries now anticipate the rise of the "ten-times bank," where a single individual leads a team of AI co-workers to deliver exponentially greater output, with growth no longer constrained by headcount but by an organization's ability to reinvent work.
---
## Contrarian Insight
The consensus view is that the Basel III Endgame re-proposal is categorically "capital friendly" for super-regionals. The 2026 capital rules also add complexity and will require careful implementation, and the operational risk model changes, internal model governance overhaul, and new output floor mechanics may consume more finance and risk capacity than the modest capital relief is worth. Super-regionals with lean capital planning teams may actually face higher total cost of compliance than their megabank peers despite technically facing lower capital requirements.
---
## Client Conversation Hooks
1. **"With SOFR NIM down sixteen basis points in April alone and the funding curve still steepening, what is your current NIM sensitivity to a further twenty-five basis points of wholesale funding cost increase, and does your FP&A model update that in real time or monthly?"**
2. **"The Basel III Endgame comment deadline is June 18 -- two weeks away. Have you modeled both the capital release scenario and the operational risk model complexity scenario, and are your regulatory capital planning systems ready for a 2027 implementation start?"**
3. **"BlackLine is calling its Q1 2026 strategy 'Agentic Financial Operations' and OneStream just took the Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader position for close and consolidation. When your bank's CFO asks why the finance close still takes eight days, what is your transformation roadmap answer?"**
---
